root_text.an4.221:1.3: Musāvādo, pisuṇā vācā, pharusā vācā, samphappalāpo—
root_text.an4.221:1.7: Saccavācā, apisuṇā vācā, saṇhā vācā, mantavācā—

translation_text.an4.221:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of bad conduct by way of speech.
translation_text.an4.221:1.3: Speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical.
translation_text.an4.221:1.4: These are the four kinds of bad conduct by way of speech.
translation_text.an4.221:1.5: There are these four kinds of good conduct by way of speech.
translation_text.an4.221:1.7: Speech that’s true, harmonious, gentle, and thoughtful.
translation_text.an4.221:1.8: These are the four kinds of good conduct by way of speech.”

variant_text.an4.221:1.7: saṇhā vācā, mantavācā → saṇhavācā mantābhassaṁ (si ), saṇhā vācā mantābhāsā (sya-all, km), saṇhavācā mantāvācā (pts1ed)
